artifacts.content:
From: Melissa A. Rodriguez <mrodriguez.jefferson@madison.k12.wi.us>
To: Enrollment Services <enrollment@badgercredit.org>
Date: 2026-02-27 09:18 AM
Subject: Re: Completed Application & Contact Info Confirmation

Hi Jenna,

Thank you for walking me through the application yesterday and for being so patient while I dug around for all those medical bill statements. I know my situation is a little complicated with the COVID-era balances and the car repair estimate, so I really appreciate your calm, step-by-step approach.

You asked me to confirm a few details so you can finalize my file. My full name is Melissa A. Rodriguez, and I’m still at the same apartment on Madison’s east side. I work as a special education teacher at Jefferson Middle School in the Madison Metropolitan School District. During school hours email is usually best, but you also needed a reliable home phone number for the paperwork.

Please list my home phone as (608) 442-9834. That’s the number you can use if you need to reach me in the evenings after 5:00 p.m., when I’m home from school and done shuttling the kids from soccer and after-school programs. If I don’t pick up right away, it just means I’m helping with homework or bedtime; leave a message and I’ll call you back the same night.

Let me know if you’re still missing anything—pay stubs, copies of Sofia’s medical invoices, or the notice about the balance that might go to collections. I want to get this consolidation set up before anything hits our credit.

Thanks again,

Melissa

Melissa A. Rodriguez
Special Education Teacher
Jefferson Middle School
Madison Metropolitan School District

metadata.subject: Re: Completed Application & Contact Info Confirmation

artifacts.content:
From: Melissa Rodriguez <mrodriguez.jefferson@madison.k12.wi.us>
To: Customer Support <support@badgercell.com>
Date: 2024-02-11 19:42
Subject: Question about changing contact number on account

Hi there,

I’m writing because I need to update the primary contact number on my BadgerCell account and I’m a little confused about which number I should list as my “home” phone.

Right now, my account still shows our old landline from years ago: (608) 555-1297. We actually disconnected that line when we moved to the east side, and I don’t want bills or automated calls going to a dead number. These days we only use our cells—mine is (608) 318-9042 and my husband Daniel’s is (608) 227-6649.

When I’m filling out school district forms or medical paperwork, they usually ask for a home phone even though we don’t technically have one. I’ve just been putting my cell, but I want to be sure that’s okay on your end and that it won’t cause any issues with two-factor authentication or billing alerts.

I’ve been trying to be more careful with all this stuff because money has been tight. When school buildings first closed, I turned down an opportunity to pick up extra virtual tutoring hours because I felt too overwhelmed learning all the new tech, and now I really wish I hadn’t because that extra income would have helped us a lot.

Can you please confirm which number you recommend I set as the main “home” contact and how to make that change online?

Thanks,
Melissa A. Rodriguez

metadata.subject: Question about changing contact number on account

artifacts.content:

To: Melissa Rodriguez <mrodriguez.jefferson@madison.k12.wi.us>
Date: 2023-11-03 10:18
Subject: Emergency Contact & Phone Numbers for Field Trips

Hi Melissa,

I’m updating the emergency contact spreadsheet for our upcoming special education community outings and I noticed that the phone number I have listed for you is the Jefferson classroom line: (608) 442-7103. That’s fine for during school hours, but administration is asking that we also keep a separate record with staff “home” or cell numbers in case something happens when we’re off campus.

Can you reply with the best number for you if we’re on a bus or at a job site? If you want Daniel listed as a secondary contact, feel free to send his number too. I know some families/teachers still use a traditional home phone, but if you only use a cell, we’ll just mark that as your home contact.

For now, I’ll keep the classroom phone as your work number and use the main office line, (608) 442-7000, as backup.

Thanks for helping me clean this up. I’m trying to get ahead of it before winter trips start and before I have to chase everyone down for missing phone info.

Best,
Carla

Carla Schmidt, RN
School Nurse, Jefferson Middle School

artifacts.content:
From: Daniel Rodriguez <drodriguez.wi@gmail.com>
To: Melissa Rodriguez <melissa.rodriguez@gmail.com>
Date: 2023-08-27 21:06
Subject: Soccer registration form – can you fill out the contact section?

Hey Mel,

I started Lucas’s fall soccer registration online, but I got stuck on the contact info page and didn’t want to mess it up.

They’re asking for a “home phone” plus separate cell numbers for each parent/guardian. I almost put my cell, (608) 227-6649, in the home phone box, but then I remembered the league last year kept calling that number during my dinner shifts and I missed half the messages.

Since you’re the one who usually handles doctor’s offices and school stuff, can you decide what we should list as the home phone? We don’t have a landline, obviously, and the form won’t let me leave it blank. Maybe use your cell there and then add mine as the secondary? There’s also a spot for an emergency contact that isn’t a parent—maybe your mom or Javier if they’ll pick up.

I saved the form with everything else filled out: address, insurance, etc. It’s just the phone section that needs fixing.

Also, there’s a waiver section asking if we consent to text alerts about weather cancellations, so whichever number you put as the home phone is probably where those will go.

Thanks,
D

metadata.subject: Soccer registration form – can you fill out the contact section?

Date: February 6, 2025 7:18 AM
Subject: Update Contact Number on Account

Hi there,

I’m writing before school starts to ask if you can update the primary phone number on our household account. Right now, I think you still have the old apartment landline on file from years ago, which we disconnected when we moved to the east side.

Please list my current cell as the main contact: 608-771-3920. I’m usually reachable by text during my prep period or right after dismissal from Jefferson Middle School. If you need a backup number, you can also add my husband Daniel’s cell: 608-555-2143.

We don’t actually have a home phone line installed in the apartment anymore—just our cells—so if there’s ever an issue with our bill or a shutoff notice, I really need it to come through one of those numbers or by email. I know I probably should have set aside more savings over the years, but I’ve never been very organized with budgeting and I tend to just deal with bills as they come in, so I rely on reminders.

If you need to verify anything, I can call from my cell during my lunch break today. Email is usually easiest, though, since I’m in and out of classrooms most of the day.

Melissa A. Rodriguez

Title: Call Spectrum to discuss internet/phone bundle
Date: March 2, 2025
Time: 4:15 PM – 4:45 PM
Location: Car (parking lot at Lucas’s soccer practice)
Attendees: Melissa Rodriguez

Notes:

- Call Spectrum customer service to ask about switching from internet-only to an internet + “home phone” bundle.
- Main goal is to see if it’s cheaper than our current setup with just my cell (608-771-3920) and Daniel’s (608-555-2143).
- Ask if the “home phone” line would be VoIP through the modem and whether we can port in an existing number. We don’t actually have a landline right now, so if they insist on assigning a random new number (like they did for Mom in Milwaukee), that’s fine as long as it doesn’t raise the price.
- Need something we can list as a home phone on school and medical forms, even if we barely use it. Right now I just put my cell everywhere, but some forms still have separate boxes for home, work, and mobile.
- Double-check that any promotional rate doesn’t jump dramatically after 12 months. If the bundle ends up more than $10/month higher than what we pay for internet alone, skip it.
- If we do sign up, ask them to email the new "home" number so I can update the kids’ school contacts and my spreadsheet later tonight.

metadata.title: Call Spectrum to discuss internet/phone bundle

Subject: UPDATED Main Office Contact Info for 2025–26

Good morning Jefferson team,

As we get ready to welcome students back, please update your records with the **revised main office contact information**. Our district telecom upgrade is complete and the school’s primary line has changed.

Effective immediately, the **new Jefferson Middle School main office phone number is (608) 555-4809**. This line will be answered from 7:15 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on school days. The old number will forward calls for the next few weeks, but families are being asked to switch to the new line as soon as possible.

Additional contact numbers:
- Attendance line: (608) 555-4820
- Student services: (608) 555-4834
- After-school program office: (608) 555-4891

Please update:
- Your email signatures
- Any classroom newsletters or syllabi
- Auto-dialer templates and Google Classroom “About” sections

Melissa, this is especially important for families of students on your caseload, since they often call the main office first when there are transportation or health issues. With the financial stress many of our families are under, we want to avoid any extra frustration from misdirected calls.

If you have printed materials with the previous phone number, you may continue to distribute them this week, but please correct the number by hand where possible.

Thank you for helping us make this a smooth transition for families.

Best,

Karen Liu
School Secretary, Jefferson Middle School

To: Melissa Rodriguez <marodriguez@madison.k12.wi.us>
Date: May 9, 2024 10:02 AM
Subject: For IEP Invitations – Use This Office Number

Hi Melissa,

Quick note while I’m thinking of it: when you send out IEP meeting invitations to families, please list **(608) 555-4827 as the Jefferson Middle School office phone**. That’s the line we’ve been using for all special education-related callbacks so we can route them directly to the case managers.

To: Enrollment Office <enrollment@charterprep.org>
Date: March 4, 2024 8:17 AM
Subject: Re: Verification of Employment and Contact Information

Hi Ms. Keller,

Thanks for following up about the employment verification form for our student transfer.

For the section asking for my school’s main phone number, please do NOT list my classroom line—that one changes when rooms get shuffled. The safest number to use is the Jefferson Middle School general office line, which our district lists on the website as (608) 204-2370. That’s the one families use to reach the front desk, and they can transfer calls to me if needed.

Sometimes outside agencies try to call the old number we had before the remodel, (608) 204-1998, and they end up in a disconnected loop. I just wanted to make sure your records don’t accidentally pick that one up from an outdated directory.

If you need a fax for any reason, the special services office still uses (608) 204-2405. I know some verification forms still have a separate line for that.

I’m trying to stay on top of all this paperwork because our finances have been really fragile since COVID. In October 2022 I even met with a bankruptcy attorney, Michael Chen, on Odana Road about possibly filing Chapter 13 over our medical and utility debt, and he warned me how losing our 2013 Honda CR‑V would affect my commute. So I’m extra careful now about anything that might delay benefits or enrollment.

Let me know if you need my direct email or principal’s contact as well.

Best,
Melissa A. Rodriguez
Special Education Teacher, Jefferson Middle School

artifacts.content:
From: Karen Thompson <kthompson@madison.k12.wi.us>
To: Jefferson MS Special Ed Team <jefferson_sped@madison.k12.wi.us>
Date: January 9, 2024 3:56 PM
Subject: Correct phone numbers for outside agency forms

Hi team,

We’ve had a couple of issues lately with outside agencies using the wrong phone number for Jefferson when they send verification forms (housing, SNAP, and tuition assistance). Please make sure you’re giving families the correct contact information.

For all forms asking for “school phone,” please provide the main office line: (608) 204-2370. Do not list your classroom extensions or the student services back line.

Some of you still have the old student services number, (608) 204-2308, saved on templates. That number technically still rings, but it bypasses the front office and confuses callers. We want everything routed through the main office first.

Melissa, you mentioned a parent was trying to use the district switchboard number, (608) 663-1875, for a landlord verification. Please follow up with her and send a new form with the correct main office phone.

I know this feels like nitpicking, but several families had benefits delayed because agencies couldn’t reach us. Given how tight finances are for many of our students’ households (and honestly for some of us, too), we really need to avoid unnecessary holdups.

Thanks for taking a few minutes to update your email signatures, letter templates, and any forms you help families complete.

Best,
Karen
Assistant Principal, Jefferson Middle School

artifacts.content:
From: Melissa Rodriguez <melissa.rodriguez@gmail.com>
To: Leasing Office <info@eastsideflats.com>
Date: August 3, 2023 9:14 AM
Subject: Inquiry about 3BR availability and application process

Hi there,

I’m a special education teacher with Madison Metropolitan School District, and my family and I are looking ahead to possibly moving next summer. We’re currently renting on the west side but considering a shift closer to the kids’ schools and my husband’s job.

Could you please let me know if you anticipate any 3-bedroom units opening for July 2024? We’re hoping for something in the $1,500–$1,700 range. For reference, our current lease at The Pines on Schroeder Road lists our address as 6720 Schroeder Rd, Apt 214, Madison, WI 53711, and our landlord is Mark Jensen. We’ve been there several years and can provide rental history.

We did have a really rough patch in March 2021 when my husband’s restaurant shut down for three weeks because of a COVID outbreak. I ended up taking a $2,000 cash advance on our Capital One card, and we even missed the April 2021 rent payment. Mark filed a 5-day notice and started an eviction case in Dane County small claims (Case No. 2021SC0XXX), but my mom wired us $600 so we could make a partial payment and set up a catch-up plan. Since then, we’ve been on time every month.

If you have a pre-application form, I’m happy to send over our current address, income verification, and references.

Best,
Melissa A. Rodriguez

metadata.subject: Inquiry about 3BR availability and application process

Title: Meet with school social worker re: family address verification
Date: August 22, 2024 3:30 PM–4:15 PM
Location: Jefferson Middle School – Student Services Office
Attendees: Melissa Rodriguez; Erin Walsh (School Social Worker)

Description:

Follow-up meeting with Erin about updated contact information for our family. Need to bring:

- Copy of current lease for 6720 Schroeder Rd, Apt 214, Madison, WI 53711 (The Pines)
- Most recent MG&E bill with our name and apartment address
- Letter from landlord, Mark Jensen, confirming we’re still month-to-month tenants and that mailing address on file is correct

Context: The district’s enrollment office flagged Lucas’s and Sofia’s records because our mailing address in Infinite Campus still shows the old east side apartment from years ago, and I never uploaded proof when we renewed. Erin said it’s fine as long as I bring documentation showing where we actually live now and that the address matches what we use for school bus routing.

Also want to ask Erin whether it’s okay that some medical billing and debt collection letters still go to my mom’s place in Milwaukee, since that’s where we had things sent during the worst of the pandemic. Need to clarify which address the school should use as our official residence versus emergency contact.

Reminder: Print out the online rent payment screen that lists “The Pines – 6720 Schroeder Rd, Apt 214” just in case they want one more proof of address.

artifacts.content:
From: Daniel Rodriguez <daniel.rodriguez83@gmail.com>
To: Melissa Rodriguez <melissa.rodriguez@gmail.com>
Date: May 16, 2022 11:47 AM
Subject: Car registration renewal – check address

Hey Mel,

Just a heads up: the DMV notice for the CR-V registration came to our old place again. They still have us listed at 1910 E Washington Ave, Apt 3B, Madison, WI 53704. I thought we changed that when we moved over to The Pines, but apparently not.

Can you go online tonight and update the mailing address to the current apartment? I’m pretty sure the insurance still shows the east side address too, and with the whole emissions test mess and the catalytic converter issue, I don’t want anything else to give them an excuse to deny a claim.

Maybe double-check what address your school has on file as well. I remember you saying that some of Sofia’s pulmonologist bills and that scary March 2021 rent notice were still bouncing around between our old apartment and your mom’s place in Milwaukee. No wonder we missed some of the statements.

If you want, I can scan the DMV letter so you have the exact wording they use for the address fields. I know you’re trying to keep everything consistent for the financial aid stuff you’re working on.

Text me at work if you need the policy number from the glove box.

Love,
Daniel

Title: Apartment viewing – Maple Grove Commons (west side)
Date: March 9, 2024 10:30 AM–11:15 AM
Location: Maple Grove Commons Leasing Office, 5101 Maple Grove Rd, Madison, WI 53719
Attendees: Melissa Rodriguez; Daniel Rodriguez

Description:

Tour 2BR and 3BR units at Maple Grove Commons. Leasing agent: "Kayla". She said to bring:

- Photo IDs
- Last two pay stubs (Jefferson MS + La Casa de Miguel)
- Current address and landlord info for rental history: The Pines, 6720 Schroeder Rd, Apt 214, Madison, WI 53711 – landlord Mark Jensen, cell number in phone

Questions to ask:

1. Exact rent and what’s included (heat? water?)
2. How strict they are about past late rent – explain briefly that in March/April 2021 we fell behind when Daniel’s restaurant shut down after a COVID outbreak, and our old landlord filed a 5-day notice and started an eviction case. We caught up after my mom wired money, but the court costs and late fees are still on our credit card.
3. Whether they require mail to be forwarded from the current address before move-in
4. Parking options for the CR-V and guest parking for family visits from Milwaukee and Chicago

Note: We’re mostly comparing commute times (Jefferson Middle School + Daniel’s job off Williamson) and seeing if the layout feels less cramped than our current apartment. Also check cell reception inside the unit and where the kids’ bus stop would be based on the address.

artifacts.content:
From: Enrollment Office <enrollment@madison.k12.wi.us>
To: Melissa Rodriguez <marodriguez@madison.k12.wi.us>
Date: July 10, 2024 2:02 PM
Subject: Address confirmation needed for Lucas and Sofia

Dear Ms. Rodriguez,

During our annual review of student records, we noticed a discrepancy between the address listed for your children and the address currently associated with your staff account.

Our system shows Lucas and Sofia as residing at 1910 E Washington Ave, Apt 3B, Madison, WI 53704. However, your employee profile lists a different mailing address on the west side of Madison. For bus routing and school assignment purposes, we must have a single verified primary residence address on file.

Please reply to this email with your current residential address and the name of your landlord or property management company. Examples of acceptable documentation include:

- A copy of your lease (e.g., for The Pines at 6720 Schroeder Rd, Apt 214, Madison, WI 53711)
- A current utility bill showing your name and address
- A letter from your landlord confirming your tenancy and address

You may redact account numbers. Documents can be emailed as scanned attachments or dropped off at the Enrollment Office.

We understand that the pandemic created housing and financial instability for many families, and we want to ensure your children remain enrolled at their current schools without interruption. If you have concerns about past-due rent, court filings, or other housing issues, please let us know; we can connect you with the district’s family resource navigator.

Sincerely,

Karen Thompson
Enrollment Specialist
Madison Metropolitan School District

metadata.subject: Address confirmation needed for Lucas and Sofia

artifacts.content:
Subject: Address confirmation for fee waiver form

Hi Melissa,

I’m finishing up the draft of your fee waiver/financial hardship statement for the district’s emergency assistance program, and I need to double‑check what mailing address you want listed.

Last year, when we submitted the utility relief paperwork, you used your mom’s place in Milwaukee as the correspondence address:

Elena Morales
4129 W. Greenfield Ave., Apt 2B
Milwaukee, WI 53215

You mentioned that was mostly because your own mail was getting mixed up with neighbors’ in your old building on the east side. Since you’ve talked about maybe moving again when your lease is up, I don’t want to accidentally put the wrong address on anything that might affect your credit or payment plans.

For this new statement, do you prefer we:
1) Use your current rental address in Madison,
2) Keep using your mom’s Milwaukee address so nothing gets lost, or
3) Use Jefferson Middle School’s address (4661 N. Blackhawk Ave., Madison, WI 53705) just for official correspondence?

Also, I’m going to reference the pandemic‑related issues you told me about—especially the CPS visit after Carla in 3B called in May 2020 and that short‑term file that closed in August 2020—because that clearly impacted your hours and ability to take on extra‑duty stipends.

Email back which address you want on the form and the exact way you want your name to appear.

Best,

Rachel Kim
School Social Worker
Jefferson Middle School

Melissa A. Rodriguez

If you have since returned to using your home address, or if you would prefer that we send statements to a different location (for example, a trusted relative such as your mother in Milwaukee), please reply with your preferred mailing address and the effective date of the change.

Notes:
Daniel and I keep going back and forth about whether it’s worth trying to move to a slightly cheaper place, even if it means a longer commute. The landlord hinted at another rent hike when the lease renews in August.

Right now, our lease lists:

Melissa & Daniel Rodriguez
Unit 4A, Lakeview Terrace Apartments
1910 E. Johnson St.
Madison, WI 53704

I need to make sure any official forms (especially anything involving our credit or the kids’ schools) are consistent. When my principal, Dr. Karen Schultz, suggested FMLA during my COVID recovery in Feb 2022, I realized how fragile everything is. artifacts.content:
Subject: Address needed for hardship documentation (pandemic impacts)

Hi Melissa,

I’m pulling together the narrative for your rental assistance application and need to plug in a consistent mailing address on the cover letter.

In your draft, you listed:

Current mailing address:
P.O. Box 6734
Madison, WI 53716

But in the attached utility bill, the service address is the apartment on the east side, and your driver’s license still shows the older address from when you lived closer to the beltline. The committee gets picky when addresses don’t match, so I want to be careful.

I’m also weaving in the specific pandemic‑related events you described: having to leave Lucas and Sofia alone for a few hours in spring 2020 when you went into Jefferson to manage IEP Zoom meetings, the CPS visit from Angela Brooks after your neighbor in 3B called, and how that short‑term case plus remote learning kept you from taking on extra‑duty stipends. Those details really help explain why you fell behind.

Can you reply confirming which address you want as your official mailing address for this application—the P.O. Box above, your current apartment, or your mom’s Milwaukee place?

Once I have that, I’ll finalize the letter and send it back for your signature.

Take care,

Marisol

Marisol Vega, MSW
Community Housing & Family Support Services

Description:
Sit down with loan officer to review:
- Current credit card balances (COVID medical, car repairs)
- Options for consolidating the UW Hospital ER bill and therapy co‑pays
- Making sure all accounts show the same home address

Background notes for myself:
After the panic attack at school in Jan 2022 and the ambulance ride, everything snowballed—ER charges, Sofia’s lung follow‑ups, and then my own COVID in Feb. Dr. Karen Schultz hinting at FMLA scared me; we couldn’t lose my paycheck with Daniel’s hours bouncing around at the restaurant.

Right now, different creditors have different addresses for us:
- Old address still on file with one card:
   875 S. Parkview Blvd., Apt 6C
   Madison, WI 53713
- Mom’s address in Milwaukee on one medical account
- Current east‑side apartment on the car insurance

I need the loan officer to help me:
1) Pick ONE official mailing address going forward
2) Set up auto‑pay so nothing goes to collections
3) Figure out if refinancing the CR‑V is even possible with our rent so high

Bring:
- Copy of lease
